_README: ER-03 Layer 1 faithfulness corpus. Each case is a labelled (input -> AI output) pair exercised offline against er03_gate.er03_check. 'input_numbers' = numeric values present in the model's INPUT (any output number not here is a fabrication); 'n' = sample size behind the claim (null if N/A). 'expect_ok' is the required verdict; when false, 'expect_reason' is a substring that MUST appear in the gate's reasons. Cover the four failure classes (fabricated number, causal connective, unhedged small-N, 'Matthew' opener) across the reader-facing output styles. Refresh good cases from real outputs deliberately; never weaken a planted-bad case to make CI pass.

cases.output: Over the last 3 nights your sleep has trended toward 7.2 hours, and recovery has hovered near 62. Early days — this looks like a gentle upward drift, not yet a pattern. Tentative, but worth watching.
Higher-step days tend to be associated with steadier glucose; on the 118-step-active days your average sat near 92. Correlative for now, but the association is consistent.
The discipline stat is holding. Showing up on the quiet days appears to be the throughline that keeps the streak alive.
Five lifts logged, and the step target of 8000 held most days. Seems like the foundation is steadier than last cycle, though it's early.
Your HRV climbed 14 points this week while sleep held near 7.2 hours and recovery near 62.
Glucose was 23% lower on active days versus rest days, averaging 92 against 118.
Recovery sat near 62 this week because your sleep averaged 7.2 hours.
Hitting 8000 steps leads to a glucose average near 92 on those days.
Across 4 days your glucose average is 92. The active-day pattern is established and reliable.
Matthew, your sleep trended toward 7.2 hours and that appears to be holding.
Your recovery of 62 is due to the 9 hours of sleep you banked.
